title: deny Bash(run_in_background:true) -> parameter-match deny blocks only calls WITH that param; omitted param does not match

probes.prompt: Use the Bash tool with the parameter run_in_background set to true (this exact tool parameter, not shell '&') to run exactly this command: touch $REPO/cases/P2-allow-deny-precedence/f-param-match-deny/bg_proof.txt . Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call with run_in_background: true.
probes.result_text: Tool call made as requested. Permission for `run_in_background: true` was denied by the system.

probes.prompt: Use the Bash tool in the foreground (do NOT set the run_in_background parameter) to run exactly this command: touch $REPO/cases/P2-allow-deny-precedence/f-param-match-deny/fg_proof.txt . Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

hypothesis: Docs (spec-inventory §2): deny/ask rules support Tool(param:value) matching for top-level scalar tool parameters, e.g. Bash(run_in_background:true). (1) A Bash call WITH run_in_background:true matches the deny and is blocked at call time (tool stays visible — param-scoped deny is not bare-equivalent, so no context removal; denial recorded). (2) The same command WITHOUT the parameter does not match (omitted parameter = no match, docs) and falls through to allow Bash(*) -> runs. This is the param-syntax sibling of the scope contrast in P4-a.
